message:
Refactor BufferFutureState polling to avoid holding locks across state transitions

The WaitingForReady branch previously borrowed shared/request inside
the match arm and held the inner service lock across both the poll and
the state transition. This restructures the logic to:

1. Extract shared (cloned) and request (taken) before entering the
   inner lock critical section.
2. Drop the inner lock before any state transition (Active, Error, or
   re-arming WaitingForReady on Pending).
3. On Pending, explicitly drop the wakers lock before restoring state,
   preventing potential deadlock if waker wake triggers re-entrancy.

The WaitingForReady arm in the outer match becomes unreachable! since
it's fully handled by the extracted if-block.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
